lede: A look at August 1 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Claudius and Dennis Lawrence, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: I-35W Mississippi River bridge
events.desc: The I-35W Mississippi River bridge spanning the Mississippi River in Minneapolis, Minnesota, collapses during the evening rush hour, killing 13 people and injuring 145.

events.title: Ycuá Bolaños supermarket fire
events.desc: A supermarket fire kills 424 people and injures 360 others in Asunción, Paraguay.

events.title: Peat
events.desc: Commercial peat-cutters discover the preserved bog body of a man, called Lindow Man, at Lindow Moss, Cheshire, England.

events.title: The Concert for Bangladesh
events.desc: The Concert for Bangladesh, organized by former Beatle George Harrison, is held at Madison Square Garden in New York City.

events.title: Harriet Quimby
events.desc: Harriet Quimby takes her pilot's test and becomes the first U.S. woman to earn an Aero Club of America aviator's certificate.

events.title: Colorado
events.desc: Colorado is admitted as the 38th U.S. state.

events.title: Lombard Street riot
events.desc: The Lombard Street riot erupts in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, United States.

events.title: First Barbary War
events.desc: First Barbary War: The American schooner USS Enterprise captures the Tripolitan polacca Tripoli in a single-ship action off the coast of modern-day Libya.

events.title: French Revolutionary Wars
events.desc: French Revolutionary Wars: Battle of the Nile (Battle of Aboukir Bay): Battle begins when a British fleet engages the French Revolutionary Navy fleet in an unusual night action.

events.title: Joseph Priestley
events.desc: British scientist Joseph Priestley discovers oxygen gas, corroborating the prior discovery of this element by German-Swedish chemist Carl Wilhelm Scheele.

births.name: Herman Melville
births.line: American novelist, short story writer, and poet

deaths.text: Corazon Aquino, Filipino politician, 11th President of the Philippines
Manuel L. Quezon, Filipino soldier, lawyer, and politician, 2nd President of the Philippines
Adhemar of Le Puy, French papal legate

observances.text: Armed Forces Day (China) or Anniversary of the Founding of the People's Liberation Army (People's Republic of China)

Emancipation Day is commemorated in many parts of the former British Empire, which marks the day the Slavery Abolition Act 1833 came into effect which abolished chattel slavery in the British Empire: Emancipation Day is a public holiday in Barbados, Bermuda, Guyana, Jamaica, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Trinidad and Tobago
Christian feast day: Abgar V of Edessa (Syrian Church)
Christian feast day: Alphonsus Maria de' Liguori
Christian feast day: Bernard Võ Văn Duệ (one of Vietnamese Martyrs)
